Read a file from S3 to use it in other s: Read a file from S3 to use it in other steps
Generate a signed URL for a file in a s3: Generate a signed URL for a file in a s3 bucket
Generate a pre-signed URL that allows an: Generate a pre-signed URL that allows anyone with the link to upload a file directly to S3 without needing AWS credentials.
Move a File to Another Folder: Move a File to Another Folder
List all files from an S3 bucket folder/: List all files from an S3 bucket folder/prefix.
Decrypt a PGP encrypted file from S3 usi: Decrypt a PGP encrypted file from S3 using a private key stored in AWS Secrets Manager
The file to upload to S3.
The name to save the file as in S3 (e.g. "report-2024.csv"). If left blank, a unique name is generated automatically.
Who can access this file after upload. U: Who can access this file after upload. Use "Private" for internal files, "Public Read" to make the file publicly accessible via URL.
Content Type of the uploaded file, if no: Content Type of the uploaded file, if not set the API will try to figure out the content type.
The full path to the file within your S3: The full path to the file within your S3 bucket (e.g. "documents/report.csv" or "myfile.txt"). This is also called the S3 "key".
How long the URL should remain valid (in: How long the URL should remain valid (in minutes).
The destination path and filename inside: The destination path and filename inside the bucket, e.g. "uploads/photo.jpg".
How long the upload URL should remain va: How long the upload URL should remain valid (in minutes).
The full path to the file you want to mo: The full path to the file you want to move (e.g. "old-folder/report.csv").
The folder path to move the file into (e: The folder path to move the file into (e.g. "archive/" or "new-folder"). The file name is kept the same.
Filter results to a specific folder. Ent: Filter results to a specific folder. Enter the folder path including the trailing slash (e.g. "invoices/" or "reports/2024/"). Leave empty to list all files in the bucket.
Maximum number of files to return (1–100: Maximum number of files to return (1–1000). Defaults to 1000. Use a lower number if you only need the most recent files.
The key (path) of the encrypted file in: The key (path) of the encrypted file in S3
The ARN of the secret in AWS Secrets Man: The ARN of the secret in AWS Secrets Manager containing the PGP private key
Optional ARN of the secret in AWS Secret: Optional ARN of the secret in AWS Secrets Manager containing the passphrase for the private key (if the key is encrypted)
The AWS region where the Secrets Manager: The AWS region where the Secrets Manager secret is stored (defaults to S3 region if not provided)
Allow decryption of messages without int: Allow decryption of messages without integrity protection (no MDC). WARNING: Without MDC, a malicious actor can silently modify the ciphertext, so the decrypted output may have been tampered with. Only enable this when working with legacy files where integrity protection was never used.
Allow decryption using a key not marked: Allow decryption using a key not marked for encryption (e.g. a signing-only key). Enable this if your PGP key was not flagged for encryption use.
Public Read/Write (anyone can view and u: Public Read/Write (anyone can view and upload)
Triggers when you add or update a file i: Triggers when you add or update a file in your bucket. The bucket/folder you choose must not contain more than 10,000 files.
